reasoning: The document contains 5 distinct major defects after deduplicating the overlapping programmatic and LLM findings regarding duplicated image content. Major issues include incorrect list semantics (forcing sequential numbering on non-sequential and alphabetical items), redundant screenshot images of extracted text, fallback rendering issues, and truncated alt text. With exactly 5 major defects, the document requires HUMAN_REVIEW for structural and visual remediation.

concerns.analysis: On page 3 of the source document, there is a textual list of basins with non-sequential numbering (Basin 1, Basin 2, Basin 3, Basin 4/5, Basin 8, Basin 9, etc.). The extraction output erroneously formats this as an 'ordered' list. Rendering this block as an ordered list (<ol>) will automatically prepend sequential numbers (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) to the items, resulting in nonsensical and incorrect numbering (e.g., item 4 will be '4. Basin 4/5:', item 5 will be '5. Basin 8:'). This is a clear numbering a
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Basin 4/5: The ditch design appears to bypass the basin
Basin 8: The diversion is shown conveying flow into the second cell
concerns.output_quote: {"type": "list", "list_type": "ordered", "items": [..., {"text": "Basin 4/5: The ditch design appears to bypass the basin"}, {"text": "Basin 8: The diversion is shown conveying flow into the second cell"}, ...]}

concerns.analysis: The HTML contains images that are screenshots of text blocks already extracted and presented as plain HTML text. On Page 1, list items regarding the 'Mine Map' and 'traffic' are extracted in an `<ol>`, but are immediately followed by an image whose alt text confirms it is a screenshot of those same requirements. A similar issue occurs on Page 4 with the text referencing '15A NCAC' being extracted as text and also appearing in the signature image block.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains a numbered list, including item 2 ('...on the Mine Map.') and item 3 ('Describe the traffic...').
concerns.output_quote:
The text is extracted into an
    element, but is immediately followed by an with the alt text: 'A partial view of a formal document featuring a numbered list of requirements regarding a Mine Map, traffic', confirming the text is duplicated as an embedded image.
concerns.location: Page 1 list and Page 4 signature block

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 3 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=3. Examples: p1: "Official letterhead from the North Carolina Depart"; p1: "A partial view of a formal document featuring a nu"; p4: "The concluding portion of a formal letter regardin". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "A. Generally speaking, the design for many sediment basins a"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=1. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 3 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (3 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ntal Quality, featuring the state seal and listing"; p1: "...list of requirements regarding a Mine Map, traffic"; p4: "... permit application, referencing regulation 15A NC"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=3 (truncated=3, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF features underlined text to emphasize important mailing details (e.g., 'Certified Mail', 'Return Receipt Requested'). This structural emphasis is completely lost in the output, as there is no marker representing the underline.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Correct list formatting by changing incorrect <ol> structures to <ul> or manually typing the markers
Remove redundant screenshot images that duplicate extracted text
Update the 3 fallback images with proper extractions or descriptions
Complete the truncated alt text for all images
Strip out retained header/footer text artifacts
